key_change: The synthesis misattributes the 2011 New Political Economy article (secondary analysis of Arrighi) as Arrighi's own fram

challenge_points.description: The synthesis misattributes the 2011 New Political Economy article (secondary analysis of Arrighi) as Arrighi's own framework from his 1994/2007 books.
All six listed sources are duplicates of the same 2011 article, exceeding 2 years old on a fast-moving topic with no post-2013 updates.
The analysis does not examine whether the 2011 article's specific claims about China-Russia positional dynamics have been empirically tested or revised since publication.

first_pass_summary: The research sessions produced an extremely narrow evidence base. Both sessions (china-rise-001 and china-rise-002) appear to have found and scored essentially the same source material: Giovanni Arrighi's work on "Systemic Cycles of Accumulation, Hegemonic Transitions." The findings reference what appears to be the same PDF/source three times per session, with slight variations in how it was catalogued. This means the research effectively rests on a single theoretical framework rather than a div…
